course_description.overview: The long course starts and finishes in Kuala Lumpur's City Center at the historical Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad clock tower building at Dataran Merdeka, passing iconic landmarks including Petronas Towers and Batu Caves[1][6].

key_segments.name: Start at Dataran Merdeka
key_segments.description: Historical city center start at Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad clock tower[6]

key_segments.name: Petronas Towers
key_segments.description: Passes the iconic Petronas Towers in the city center[1]

key_segments.name: Batu Caves
key_segments.description: Route passes the famous Batu Caves landmark outside the city[1]

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Competitive long course of approximately 138 km suitable for ranking and podium awards; medium course non-competitive[1][6].

logistics.getting_there: Kuala Lumpur is a major international hub with direct flights to Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KUL); city center accessible by train, taxi or ride-share[1].
logistics.accommodation: Wide range from luxury hotels near Petronas Towers to budget options; shopping malls and street food abundant[1].
logistics.registration_details: Register via official GFNY Kuala Lumpur site; all riders receive official jersey (mandatory); chip timing with results at gfny.cc/results[1].
logistics.bike_logistics: Fly with bike or rent locally; personalized training plans available from GFNY Coaches[1].
logistics.local_considerations: Tropical climate with heat and humidity; rich culture, food, and shopping; business center of Asia[1].

final_verdict.one_liner: Iconic GFNY debut in Malaysia blending urban landmarks with cultural adventure.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: GFNY enthusiasts seeking Asian prestige, qualifiers aiming for top 10% age group, or tourists combining race with KL exploration.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes for GFNY series participants or those wanting a scenic, competitive road event in Southeast Asia.
pro_tips: Train for heat and urban traffic
